subroutines.name: language_startup
subroutines.title: Language startup
subroutines.description:
Reached from the language entry when the MOS starts BASIC
(A = 1). Reads HIMEM and PAGE from the MOS, clears the print and
formatting state, seeds the random-number generator if it is cold,
installs the BASIC error handler in BRKV, and jumps to
start_new_program, which clears any program and enters the
immediate ("> ") loop.

on_exit.control: jumps to start_new_program (does not return)

subroutines.name: keyword_table
subroutines.title: Keyword / tokeniser table
subroutines.description:
Each entry is the keyword in ASCII, then a token byte (bit 7
set), then a flag byte that drives tokenising. The flag bits are:

| Bit | Meaning |
|-----|---------|
| 0 | Conditional: do not tokenise if followed by a letter |
| 1 | Enter "middle of statement" mode |
| 2 | Enter "start of statement" mode |
| 3 | FN/PROC: do not tokenise the following name |
| 4 | Start tokenising a line number (after GOTO etc.) |
| 5 | Do not tokenise the rest of the line (REM, DATA) |
| 6 | Pseudo-variable: add &40 to the token in a statement |

Bit 6 is why a pseudo-variable like PTR reads as &8F in a function
position but &CF as an assignment target. Entries are ordered so that
the first acceptable abbreviation of each keyword is unambiguous.

The tokeniser scan stops at WIDTH's token &FE, which doubles as the
end-of-table sentinel (see the `cmp #&fe` in the keyword search). The
final five entries — the PTR/PAGE/TIME/LOMEM/HIMEM *assignment* forms
&CF-&D3 — sit past that sentinel, so the tokeniser never matches them
(it produces those tokens arithmetically via bit 6). They exist only
for the de-tokeniser (`print_token`, which has no sentinel and scans by
token value) to render `PTR=` etc. back to text. The data then runs on
to the action-address tables at action_table_lo.

subroutines.name: tokenise_line
subroutines.title: Tokenise a line
subroutines.description:
Convert the line being entered into its internal form, replacing
keywords with tokens via the keyword_table while leaving strings,
line numbers and names intact. Works through the buffer using the
general pointer (zp_general, &37).

Three entry points share this scanner, differing only in how much of
the &3B/&3C state they reset:
 - tokenise_line (&8951): start a fresh line; clear both the
   start-of-statement flag &3B and the line-number flag &3C.
 - tokenise_resume (&8955): clear only &3C; the caller has already set
   &3B. EVAL enters here with &3B = mid-statement so a re-tokenised
   string reads PTR etc. as functions, not assignment targets.
 - tok_scan (&8957): clear neither; execute_line enters here having
   pre-armed &3C = &FF so a typed leading line number is encoded with
   the &8D token (then recovered by check_line_number, see &8B2D).

on_entry.zp_general (&37/&38): a pointer to the line text to tokenise

on_exit.(zp_general): the line rewritten with keyword tokens

subroutines.name: statement_loop
subroutines.title: Statement execution loop
subroutines.description:
The main interpreter loop: fetch the next statement's leading
token and dispatch it, then advance to the next statement (after a
colon) or line. Statement handlers jmp back here when they finish, so
it is the common continuation rather than a callable subroutine.

on_entry.zp_text_ptr_off (&0A): offset at the next statement

on_exit.control: dispatches the statement; handlers return here

subroutines.name: dispatch_token
subroutines.title: Dispatch a tokenised function or command
subroutines.description:
Index the action-address table by (token - &8E): load the
handler address into zp_general (&37/&38) and JMP (&0037). This is
the indirect jump that reaches every fn_* / stmt_* handler.

subroutines.name: try_variable_assignment
subroutines.title: Not a command token: try an assignment
subroutines.description:
Reached when the statement does not begin with a command token:
copy PtrA to PtrB, parse a variable / indirection reference, and perform
an implied-LET assignment (creating the variable if new). Falls back to
the =, * (OSCLI) or [ (assembler) special statement forms.

on_exit.control: performs the assignment then rejoins statement_loop, or dispatches to the =, * or [ form

subroutines.name: assign_string
subroutines.title: Store a string value into a variable
subroutines.description:
Assign the string in the string buffer to the string variable
whose descriptor address is on the stack. Reuses the existing
allocation if the new string fits, otherwise grabs fresh space from
the heap.

subroutines.name: unstack_value_to_var
subroutines.title: Restore a stacked value into a variable
subroutines.description: Pop the value on top of the BASIC stack into the variable at zp_general, dispatched by the type/size byte: a numeric value of that many bytes, a $addr string (CR terminated), or a string variable (copied into its heap allocation). Used by the FN/PROC LOCAL and parameter machinery.

on_entry.(zp_stack_ptr) (&04/&05): the saved value on top of stack

subroutines.name: print_special_item
subroutines.title: Handle the PRINT ' TAB and SPC items
subroutines.description:
Recognise and act on the special PRINT items: apostrophe
(force a newline), TAB(x[,y]) and SPC(n). Returns carry clear when
it consumed one, so the caller skips ordinary expression printing.

on_entry.zp_text_ptr_off (&0A): offset at the PRINT item

on_exit.C: clear if a special item was consumed and printed, else set

subroutines.name: usr_call
subroutines.title: Call user machine code (USR/CALL)
subroutines.description:
Load A, X, Y and the carry from the resident integer
variables A%, X%, Y% and C%, then JMP (IWA) to the user routine. Its RTS
returns to usr_call's caller, which captures the registers back.

on_exit.A: as left by the called code
on_exit.X: as left by the called code
on_exit.Y: as left by the called code
on_exit.P: as left by the called code

subroutines.name: advance_to_next_line
subroutines.title: Advance the general pointer to the next program line
subroutines.description: Add the line length byte (at offset 3 of the current line) to zp_general so it addresses the next program line. Clears carry for the caller.

on_entry.zp_general (&37/&38): a pointer to the current line
on_entry.C: clear (it is the carry-in to the add)

subroutines.name: iwa_inc
subroutines.title: Increment the integer accumulator
subroutines.description: IWA = IWA + 1, carrying through all four bytes.

subroutines.name: imul16
subroutines.title: 16-bit integer multiply (IWA * FWB)
subroutines.description: Multiply IWA by the 16-bit multiplier in the FWB mantissa (m2/m3) by shift-and-add, leaving the 16-bit product in IWA low/high. Raises Too big on overflow. Used for array subscript arithmetic.

subroutines.name: eval_comma_integer
subroutines.title: Evaluate a comma-separated integer argument
subroutines.description:
Skip a comma at PtrB (Missing , if absent), then fall into
eval_expr_integer to evaluate the following expression as an integer.

subroutines.name: eval_expr_integer
subroutines.title: Evaluate an expression as an integer
subroutines.description:
Evaluate the expression at PtrB (eval_or_eor) and coerce the
result to an integer (coerce_to_integer). Unlike eval_expr_to_integer
it does not sync the primary pointer, so the caller keeps managing PtrB.

subroutines.name: eval_factor_integer
subroutines.title: Evaluate a single factor as an integer
subroutines.description:
Evaluate one factor at PtrB (eval_factor, so without applying
any operators) and coerce it to an integer: a string raises Type
mismatch, a real is converted, an integer is returned unchanged.

subroutines.name: eval_eq_integer
subroutines.title: Require "=" then evaluate an integer
subroutines.description:
Copy PtrA to PtrB and require an "=" (sub_c9807), evaluate the
value that follows, then fall into coerce_var_to_integer. Used by the
pseudo-variable assignments (TIME=, PAGE=, ...).

on_exit.BRK: Mistake if "=" is missing, Type mismatch on a string

subroutines.name: coerce_var_to_integer
subroutines.title: Coerce the current value to an integer
subroutines.description:
Load the value type from zp_var_type and fall into
coerce_to_integer, converting the just-evaluated value to an integer.
The entry to use when the type is in zp_var_type rather than A.

subroutines.name: coerce_to_integer
subroutines.title: Coerce the current value to an integer
subroutines.description:
Check the type of the last evaluated value (in A from
zp_var_type): a string raises "Type mismatch", an integer returns
unchanged, and a real is converted to a 4-byte integer in the
integer accumulator.

subroutines.name: eval_real
subroutines.title: Evaluate an expression as a real
subroutines.description: Evaluate a factor at PtrB and ensure the result is real, converting an integer with int_to_fwa.

on_entry.zp_text_ptr2 (&19/&1A): PtrB at the factor to evaluate

on_exit.A: real type marker (negative)

subroutines.name: ensure_real
subroutines.title: Coerce the result to a real
subroutines.description: Leave a real unchanged, convert an integer to FWA (int_to_fwa), or raise Type mismatch for a string.

on_entry.A: the value type (negative real, positive integer, 0 string)

on_exit.A: real type marker (negative)

subroutines.name: find_proc_fn
subroutines.title: Find a PROC or FN definition by name
subroutines.description:
Search the PROC (token &F2) or FN linked list for a definition
whose name starts at (zp_general)+1. Shares the chain walk with
find_variable; returns a pointer to the body, or "not found".

on_entry.zp_fileblk (&39): the length of the name being sought

on_exit.zp_iwa (&2A/&2B): pointer to the definition body (when found)
on_exit.Z: clear if found, set if not present

subroutines.name: find_variable
subroutines.title: Find a variable by name
subroutines.description:
Search the heap for a variable whose name starts at
(zp_general)+1. The initial character selects one of the per-letter
linked lists via the variable table; the chain is walked comparing
the rest of the name. On a match, returns a pointer to the value in
zp_iwa/zp_iwa_1; otherwise reports it is not present.

on_entry.zp_fileblk (&39): the length of the name being sought

on_exit.Z: clear if found, set if not present

subroutines.name: create_variable
subroutines.title: Create a new variable
subroutines.description: Append a new variable entry at VARTOP: walk to the end of the per-letter chain, link in the new node, and copy the name into it. The caller initialises the value bytes (clear_value_bytes) and advances VARTOP (sub_c9539).

on_exit.(zp_vartop): a new linked entry with its name
on_exit.Y: offset of the last name byte in the entry

subroutines.name: clear_value_bytes
subroutines.title: Zero a run of value bytes
subroutines.description: Write X zero bytes after a new variable name at (zp_vartop),Y, initialising its value to zero.

on_entry.X: the number of value bytes to clear
on_entry.zp_vartop (&02/&03): base of the new variable entry
on_entry.Y: offset of the last name byte

subroutines.name: validate_var_name
subroutines.title: Validate / measure a variable name
subroutines.description: Scan the variable name at (zp_general)+1, stopping at the first non-name character (letters, digits and _ are accepted; a leading digit is rejected, giving an empty name). Advances X as the running text offset.

on_entry.(zp_general) (&37/&38): a pointer to the name (name at +1)

on_exit.Y: one past the name (Y-1 = length; Y=1 means invalid)
on_exit.X: the text offset advanced past the name
on_exit.A: the terminating non-name character

subroutines.name: parse_lvalue
subroutines.title: Parse an assignment target variable
subroutines.description:
Parse the variable reference being assigned to (parse_var_ref)
and return a pointer to its storage plus its type, creating the variable
(create_variable + clear_value_bytes) if it does not yet exist. Shared
by LET and FOR.

on_entry.zp_text_ptr_off (&0A): offset of the target reference

on_exit.zp_iwa (&2A/&2B): a pointer to the variable storage

subroutines.name: parse_var_ref
subroutines.title: Parse a variable reference / lvalue
subroutines.description: Scan a variable name, array element, or indirection operator at the text pointer. Sets the value address in &2A/&2B and a type byte in &2C: 0=? byte indirection, 4=integer (also ! word indirection), 5=real, &80=$ indirection, &81=string lvalue. Returns A nonzero when resolved; A=0 carry clear for a valid name not yet defined (caller creates it), A=0 carry set when no name is present.

on_entry.zp_text_ptr_off (&0A): offset of the reference

on_exit.A: nonzero if resolved, 0 if a name needs creating / absent
on_exit.C: with A=0: clear = create the name, set = no name

subroutines.name: index_array
subroutines.title: Locate an array and evaluate one subscript
subroutines.description: Find the named array (find_variable) and step the value pointer to the addressed element, evaluating one subscript at PtrB and bounds-checking it. Raises Array on a missing array, Subscript on an out-of-range index.

subroutines.name: check_subscript_bound
subroutines.title: Check a subscript against a dimension extent
subroutines.description: Raise Subscript if the subscript in IWA is negative or not less than the two-byte dimension extent at (zp_general),Y, otherwise advance Y past the extent.

on_entry.zp_general (&37/&38): a pointer to the dimension data

subroutines.name: check_line_number
subroutines.title: Test for an embedded line number
subroutines.description: Skip spaces at the text pointer; if the next token is a line-number token (&8D), fall into decode_line_number to decode it into IWA and return carry set, otherwise return carry clear.

on_entry.zp_text_ptr_off (&0A): offset of the next character

on_exit.C: set if a line number was found and decoded

subroutines.name: decode_line_number
subroutines.title: Decode a 3-byte line number into IWA
subroutines.description: Reverse the three-byte &8D line-number encoding, reconstructing the 16-bit line number in IWA (the two high-bit pairs are recovered from the control byte). The inverse of encode_line_number, and like it exact only for 0-32767.

subroutines.name: eval_after_eq
subroutines.title: Expect "=" then evaluate the right-hand side
subroutines.description:
Skip spaces at PtrB, require an "=" sign, then evaluate the
expression that follows, leaving the value in the accumulator with its
type in zp_var_type. Raises Mistake if "=" is missing.

on_exit.A: the result type (also in zp_var_type, &27)

subroutines.name: expect_eq
subroutines.title: Require "=" at the parser pointer
subroutines.description: Skip spaces at PtrB and raise Mistake unless the next character is "=".

on_exit.A: the "=" character (consumed)

subroutines.name: check_end_of_statement
subroutines.title: Check for end of statement
subroutines.description:
Skip spaces and require the statement to end here: a colon, an
end-of-line (&0D), or ELSE. Anything else raises "Syntax error".
Also polls for Escape.

on_entry.zp_text_ptr_off (&0A): offset of the next character

on_exit.A: the terminator character
on_exit.BRK: Syntax error if more text follows

subroutines.name: trace_line
subroutines.title: Print [line] when TRACE is active
subroutines.description: If the line number in IWA is at or below the TRACE ceiling, print it in brackets; otherwise do nothing.

subroutines.name: print_line_number
subroutines.title: Print a 16-bit line number in decimal
subroutines.description: Convert the value in IWA to decimal by repeated subtraction of the powers of ten at &996B/&99B9, suppressing leading zeros, optionally right-justified in a field (the sub_c9923 entry uses a 5-digit TRACE field).

subroutines.name: find_program_line
subroutines.title: Search the program for a line number
subroutines.description:
Walk the program text from PAGE looking for the target line
number in IWA. Returns a scratch pointer (zp_fwb_exp/zp_fwb_m1) to the
matching line, or to the first line with a greater number (the insertion
point) when there is no exact match.

on_entry.zp_page (&18): PAGE, the start of the program

on_exit.(zp_fwb_exp) (&3D/&3E): pointer to the line (or insertion point)
on_exit.C: clear if an exact match was found, set if not found

subroutines.name: iwa_divide
subroutines.title: Unsigned/signed 32-bit integer division
subroutines.description: Divide the dividend (IWA, the left operand) by the divisor (the right operand, evaluated here at PtrB). Both are made positive and a 32-step shift-subtract division runs with the dividend/quotient in &39-&3C and the remainder in &3D-&40. The quotient sign is the XOR of the operand signs (&37), the remainder sign the dividend sign (&38). Raises Division by zero. Shared by DIV (reads the quotient) and MOD (the remainder).

subroutines.name: fp_compare
subroutines.title: Compare FWA with a fp variable
subroutines.description: Unpack the packed real operand into FWB and compare it with FWA by sign, exponent and mantissa, returning the ordering for the relational operators.

on_exit.Z: set if the values are equal
on_exit.C: the sign-corrected ordering when they differ
on_exit.A: nonzero when they differ

subroutines.name: eval_and_compare
subroutines.title: Evaluate the next operand and compare
subroutines.description: Stack the already-evaluated left value, evaluate the next arithmetic operand at PtrB, then compare the two (integer, real or string), returning the ordering flags for the relational operators.

on_entry.A: the type of the left value (already evaluated)

on_exit.C: the ordering of left vs right

subroutines.name: iwa_test_var
subroutines.title: Compare an integer variable with the accumulator
subroutines.description: Signed 32-bit compare of the integer on top of the BASIC stack against IWA: bias both sign bits so an unsigned subtract orders signed values, then subtract, returning the ordering in C and Z (Z = equal).

subroutines.name: eval_expr
subroutines.title: Evaluate an expression
subroutines.description:
The interpreter's main expression entry point. Copies the
primary text pointer (PtrA: zp_text_ptr / zp_text_ptr_off) to the
secondary pointer (PtrB: zp_text_ptr2 / &1B) and evaluates the
expression there.

subroutines.name: eval_or_eor
subroutines.title: Evaluator level 7: OR, EOR
subroutines.description: Lowest precedence: bitwise OR (&84) and EOR (&82) on integers.

on_entry.zp_text_ptr2_off (&1B): offset into the text

on_exit.A: result type: <0 = float in fwa, >0 = integer in iwa, 0 = string
on_exit.zp_iwa / zp_fwa / string_work (&0600): the value, selected by the type in A

subroutines.name: eval_and
subroutines.title: Evaluator level 6: AND
subroutines.description: Bitwise AND (&80) on integers.

on_entry.zp_text_ptr2_off (&1B): offset into the text

on_exit.A: result type: <0 = float in fwa, >0 = integer in iwa, 0 = string
on_exit.zp_iwa / zp_fwa / string_work (&0600): the value, selected by the type in A

subroutines.name: eval_relational
subroutines.title: Evaluator level 5: < <= = >= > <>
subroutines.description: The relational operators, yielding TRUE (-1) or FALSE (0).

on_entry.zp_text_ptr2_off (&1B): offset into the text

on_exit.A: result type: <0 = float in fwa, >0 = integer in iwa, 0 = string
on_exit.zp_iwa / zp_fwa / string_work (&0600): the value, selected by the type in A

subroutines.name: eval_add_sub
subroutines.title: Evaluator level 4: + -
subroutines.description: Addition and subtraction (numeric, or string concatenation).

on_entry.zp_text_ptr2_off (&1B): offset into the text

on_exit.A: result type: <0 = float in fwa, >0 = integer in iwa, 0 = string
on_exit.zp_iwa / zp_fwa / string_work (&0600): the value, selected by the type in A

subroutines.name: eval_mul_div
subroutines.title: Evaluator level 3: * / DIV MOD
subroutines.description: Multiplication, division and the integer DIV and MOD operators.

on_entry.zp_text_ptr2_off (&1B): offset into the text

on_exit.A: result type: <0 = float in fwa, >0 = integer in iwa, 0 = string
on_exit.zp_iwa / zp_fwa / string_work (&0600): the value, selected by the type in A

subroutines.name: iwa_add
subroutines.title: Integer add
subroutines.description: IWA = IWA + the integer operand on the BASIC stack.

on_entry.(zp_stack_ptr) (&04): the other operand on the BASIC stack

subroutines.name: iwa_rsub
subroutines.title: Reverse integer subtract
subroutines.description: IWA = stacked operand - IWA.

on_entry.(zp_stack_ptr) (&04): the minuend on the BASIC stack

subroutines.name: iwa_mul
subroutines.title: Integer multiply
subroutines.description:
IWA = IWA * the stacked operand. A product wider than
16 bits is truncated to 16 significant bits.

on_entry.(zp_stack_ptr) (&04): the other factor on the BASIC stack

subroutines.name: iwa_mod
subroutines.title: Integer remainder
subroutines.description:
IWA = IWA MOD the integer operand. Raises "Division by
zero" if the divisor is zero.

subroutines.name: iwa_div
subroutines.title: Integer divide
subroutines.description:
IWA = IWA DIV the integer operand. Raises "Division by
zero" if the divisor is zero.

subroutines.name: eval_power
subroutines.title: Expression Level 2 - the ^ operator
subroutines.description: Evaluate a factor, then for each ^ raise it to the power: an integer exponent uses repeated multiplication, otherwise x^y = x^int * exp(frac * ln x).

on_entry.zp_text_ptr2_off (&1B): offset into the text

on_exit.A: result type: <0 = float in fwa, >0 = integer in iwa, 0 = string
on_exit.zp_iwa / zp_fwa / string_work (&0600): the value, selected by the type in A

subroutines.name: number_to_ascii
subroutines.title: Convert the current value to an ASCII number
subroutines.description:
Convert the integer (IWA) or real (FWA) value to an ASCII
string in the string work area, in decimal or hex per the radix
flag and the @% print format. Underlies PRINT and STR$.

subroutines.name: output_top_digit
subroutines.title: Output the leading decimal digit of FWA
subroutines.description: Emit the integer part (top nibble of the mantissa MSB) as a decimal digit, mask it off, then multiply the remaining fraction by 10 ready for the next digit. The inner step of the real-to-ASCII conversion.

on_entry.zp_fwa_m1 (&31): mantissa MSB; top nibble is the digit

on_exit.zp_fwa (&31-&35): fraction times ten for the next digit

subroutines.name: output_byte_decimal
subroutines.title: Output a byte (0-99) in decimal
subroutines.description: Append a byte 0-99 to the output string as one or two decimal digits (a leading-zero tens digit is dropped) by repeated subtraction of ten.

subroutines.name: output_digit
subroutines.title: Output A as a decimal digit (A + "0")
subroutines.description: Convert the digit value in A to ASCII ("0" + A) and append it via output_char.

subroutines.name: output_char
subroutines.title: Append a character to the output string
subroutines.description: Append the character in A to the end of the output string buffer and bump its length.

on_exit.string_work (&0600): A stored at the old length

subroutines.name: parse_number
subroutines.title: Parse an unsigned number at PtrB
subroutines.description: Parse an unsigned decimal or & hex number at PtrB into the accumulator, choosing integer or real form (a decimal point or E exponent forces real).

on_exit.A: result type: <0 float in fwa, >0 integer in iwa

subroutines.name: parse_exponent
subroutines.title: Parse the "E" exponent (optional sign, 1-2 digits)
subroutines.description: Read the one- or two-digit decimal exponent after "E" at PtrB, returning its magnitude in A (0 if no digits). The sign is handled on a separate entry path; the caller adds the result to the FWA exponent.

on_exit.A: the exponent magnitude (0-99)
on_exit.C: clear (ready for the caller to add it)

subroutines.name: fwa_acc_fwb
subroutines.title: Add FWB into FWA
subroutines.description: Add the FWB mantissa (rnd, m4-m1) into the FWA mantissa with a single carry chain. Mantissa-only: exponents must already be aligned by the caller. Used by multiply, *10 and /10.

on_entry.C: clear (it is the carry-in to the addition)

on_exit.C: carry out of the mantissa MSB (overflow)

subroutines.name: mant_mul10
subroutines.title: Multiply the FWA mantissa by 10
subroutines.description: Multiply the FWA mantissa (m1-m4 and the rounding byte) by ten as x4 + x1 = x5 then x2, leaving the exponent untouched. Used by the number<->ASCII conversions, which track the decimal point separately.

on_exit.C: carry out of the mantissa MSB
on_exit.A: preserved

subroutines.name: fwa_sign
subroutines.title: Get the sign of the FP accumulator
subroutines.description:
Determine the sign of the floating-point accumulator by
OR-ing the mantissa and rounding bytes (&31-&35) to test for zero, then
reading the sign byte. Forces a clean zero (sign, exponent and overflow
byte cleared) when the mantissa is empty.

on_exit.A: +1 positive, 0 zero, &FF negative

subroutines.name: fwa_mul10
subroutines.title: FWA = FWA * 10
subroutines.description: Multiply FWA by ten as x8 + x2: scale the exponent by eight, build x2 in FWB, and add. Left unnormalised and unrounded (the caller normalises). Uses FWB as scratch.

subroutines.name: fwb_copy_from_fwa
subroutines.title: FWB = FWA
subroutines.description: Copy all ten bytes of FWA into FWB.

subroutines.name: fwb_div2
subroutines.title: Divide FWB by two
subroutines.description: Shift the FWB mantissa right one bit (FWB = FWB / 2). Leaves the exponent alone, so the result is left de-normalised; used to align exponents before adding.

on_exit.zp_fwb: mantissa shifted right one bit (rnd byte included)
on_exit.C: the bit shifted out of the rnd byte

subroutines.name: fwa_div10
subroutines.title: FWA = FWA / 10
subroutines.description: Divide FWA by ten using the binary expansion of 1/10 (x/16 plus progressively shifted terms accumulated via FWB). Left unnormalised and unrounded (the caller normalises). Uses FWB as scratch.

subroutines.name: fwa_round_carry
subroutines.title: Add to the rounding byte and ripple the carry up
subroutines.description:
Add A (plus the carry-in) to the FWA rounding byte, then
propagate any carry up through the mantissa (m4 -> m1). A carry out of
the top renormalises the exponent (shift right, exponent + 1) and may
set the overflow byte. Used to round the mantissa up.

on_entry.A: the rounding increment to add to the rnd byte

on_exit.zp_fwa (&30-&35): the rounded mantissa (exponent may step up)

subroutines.name: int_to_fwa
subroutines.title: Convert the integer accumulator to floating point
subroutines.description: Convert the signed 32-bit integer in IWA to a normalised real in FWA: record the sign (negating IWA if needed), copy the magnitude into the mantissa with exponent 32, and normalise.

on_exit.zp_fwa (&2E-&35): the value as a normalised real
on_exit.zp_iwa: made positive if it was negative

subroutines.name: small_int_to_fwa
subroutines.title: Convert a small (8-bit) integer in A to FWA
subroutines.description: Convert the signed 8-bit integer in A to a normalised real in FWA (clears FWA, places the magnitude in the mantissa MSB with exponent 8, sets the sign, and normalises).

on_exit.zp_fwa (&2E-&35): the value as a normalised real

subroutines.name: fwa_normalise
subroutines.title: Normalise FWA
subroutines.description: Shift the FWA mantissa left until bit 7 of the MSB is set, decrementing the exponent by 8 per whole-byte shift and by 1 per bit, so the implied leading 1 is restored. An all-zero mantissa is forced to a clean zero. Underflow is handled via the overflow byte.

on_exit.zp_fwa (&2E-&35): normalised (mantissa MSB bit 7 set) or a clean zero

subroutines.name: fwb_unpack_var
subroutines.title: Unpack a fp variable into FWB
subroutines.description: Expand the packed five-byte float addressed by zp_fp_ptr into the unpacked FWB accumulator: split the sign out of the mantissa MSB, restore the implied leading 1 (unless the value is zero), and clear the overflow and rounding bytes.

subroutines.name: fwa_pack_temp2
subroutines.title: Pack FWA into TEMP2
subroutines.description: Point zp_fp_ptr at FP TEMP2 (&0471), then pack FWA into it via fwa_pack_var.

subroutines.name: fwa_pack_temp3
subroutines.title: Pack FWA into TEMP3
subroutines.description: Point zp_fp_ptr at FP TEMP3 (&0476), then pack FWA into it via fwa_pack_var.

subroutines.name: fwa_pack_temp1
subroutines.title: Pack FWA into TEMP1
subroutines.description: Point zp_fp_ptr at FP TEMP1 (&046C), then pack FWA into it via fwa_pack_var.

subroutines.name: fwa_pack_var
subroutines.title: Pack FWA into a fp variable
subroutines.description: Compress the unpacked FWA accumulator into the packed five-byte form addressed by zp_fp_ptr: exponent, then the sign folded into bit 7 of the mantissa MSB (the implied leading 1 is dropped), then mantissa bytes 2-4.

on_entry.zp_fp_ptr (&4B/&4C): where to write the five bytes

subroutines.name: fwa_unpack_temp1
subroutines.title: Unpack TEMP1 into FWA
subroutines.description: Point zp_fp_ptr at FP TEMP1 (&046C), then unpack it into FWA via fwa_unpack_var.

subroutines.name: fwa_unpack_var
subroutines.title: Unpack a floating-point variable into FWA
subroutines.description:
Expand the packed five-byte float addressed by zp_fp_ptr into
the unpacked FWA accumulator: split the sign out of the mantissa MSB,
restore the implied leading 1 (unless the value is zero), and clear the
overflow and rounding bytes.

subroutines.name: fwa_to_int
subroutines.title: Convert the FP accumulator to an integer
subroutines.description: Convert FWA to a 4-byte integer in IWA by denormalising (fwa_to_int2) and copying the mantissa into IWA. Truncates toward zero.

subroutines.name: fwa_to_int2
subroutines.title: Denormalise FWA to a fixed-point integer
subroutines.description: Shift the FWA mantissa so it represents the integer part at a fixed scale (exponent &A0), pushing the fractional bits out into FWB. The shared core of fwa_to_int and fp_split_int_frac; the caller reads the integer from the mantissa.

on_exit.zp_fwa (&31-&34): the integer part in the mantissa

subroutines.name: fwb_clear
subroutines.title: FWB = 0
subroutines.description: Zero every byte of FWB. Exponent 0 is the special encoding for the value zero.

subroutines.name: fp_split_int_frac
subroutines.title: Split FWA into integer part (&4A) and fraction (FWA)
subroutines.description: Round FWA to the nearest integer, leaving that integer in &4A and the signed remainder (in [-0.5, 0.5]) in FWA. Used by EXP to separate the integer and fractional powers.

subroutines.name: fwa_sub_var
subroutines.title: FWA = FWA - fp var
subroutines.description: Subtract the packed real operand from FWA: compute operand - FWA (fwa_rsub_var) then negate the result.

subroutines.name: fwa_swap_var
subroutines.title: Swap FWA and a fp variable
subroutines.description: Exchange FWA with the packed real at zp_fp_ptr: unpack the operand into FWB, pack FWA into the variable, then copy FWB into FWA.

subroutines.name: fwa_copy_from_fwb
subroutines.title: FWA = FWB
subroutines.description: Copy all ten bytes of FWB into FWA.

subroutines.name: fwa_rsub_var
subroutines.title: FWA = fp var - FWA
subroutines.description: Reverse subtract: negate FWA then add the packed real operand, giving operand - FWA (normalised, rounded).

subroutines.name: fwa_add_var
subroutines.title: FWA = FWA + fp var
subroutines.description: Unpack the packed real operand into FWB and add it to FWA (normalised, rounded).

subroutines.name: fwa_add_fwb
subroutines.title: FWA = FWA + FWB
subroutines.description: Add FWB to FWA (fwa_add_fwb_raw then fwa_round), normalised and rounded.

subroutines.name: fwa_add_fwb_raw
subroutines.title: FWA = FWA + FWB (unrounded)
subroutines.description: Add FWB to FWA: align the smaller operand to the larger exponent, add the mantissas and normalise. Left unrounded (the caller rounds). Operands differing by >= 37 bits leave FWA unchanged.

subroutines.name: fwa_mul_var_raw
subroutines.title: FWA = FWA * fp var (raw)
subroutines.description: Multiply FWA by the packed real operand (sign XOR, exponents added, mantissas multiplied by shift-and-add). A zero operand gives zero. Left unnormalised and unrounded (the caller finishes).

subroutines.name: fwa_mul_var
subroutines.title: FWA = FWA * fp var
subroutines.description: Multiply FWA by the packed real operand (fwa_mul_var_raw then normalise and round).

subroutines.name: fwa_round
subroutines.title: Round FWA
subroutines.description: Round FWA to the mantissa LSB using the rounding byte: round to nearest, ties handled by the LSB. A carry out of the top renormalises and can raise Too big.

on_entry.zp_fwa (&2E-&35): the value to round (rnd byte holds the bits below the LSB)

subroutines.name: fwa_clear
subroutines.title: FWA = 0
subroutines.description: Zero every byte of FWA. Exponent 0 is the special encoding for the value zero.

subroutines.name: fwa_set_one
subroutines.title: FWA = 1
subroutines.description: Set the floating-point accumulator to 1.0 (mantissa MSB &80, exponent &81).

on_exit.A: nonzero (flags a real result)

subroutines.name: fwa_reciprocal
subroutines.title: FWA = 1 / FWA
subroutines.description: Reciprocal: save FWA in TEMP1, set FWA = 1, then divide by the saved value (normalised, rounded). Raises Division by zero if FWA was zero.

on_exit.BRK: Division by zero if FWA was zero

subroutines.name: fwa_rdiv_var
subroutines.title: FWA = fp var / FWA
subroutines.description: Reverse divide: copy FWA (the divisor) into FWB, unpack the packed real operand (the dividend) into FWA, then divide, giving operand / FWA. Raises Division by zero if FWA is zero.

on_exit.BRK: Division by zero if FWA was zero

subroutines.name: fp_divide
subroutines.title: FWA = FWA / divisor (restoring long division)
subroutines.description: Floating-point divide of FWA by the packed real divisor: sign XOR, exponents subtracted, then 32 quotient bits plus guard bits by restoring long division (compare/subtract/shift). Raises Division by zero on a zero divisor; a zero dividend gives zero.

on_exit.BRK: Division by zero on a zero divisor

subroutines.name: point_fp_temp4
subroutines.title: Point zp_fp_ptr at FP TEMP4 (&047B)
subroutines.description: Set zp_fp_ptr to FP TEMP4, ready for a pack/unpack.

subroutines.name: point_fp_temp2
subroutines.title: Point zp_fp_ptr at FP TEMP2 (&0471)
subroutines.description: Set zp_fp_ptr to FP TEMP2, ready for a pack/unpack.

subroutines.name: point_fp_temp3
subroutines.title: Point zp_fp_ptr at FP TEMP3 (&0476)
subroutines.description: Set zp_fp_ptr to FP TEMP3, ready for a pack/unpack.

subroutines.name: point_fp_temp1
subroutines.title: Point zp_fp_ptr at FP TEMP1 (&046C)
subroutines.description: Set zp_fp_ptr to FP TEMP1, ready for a pack/unpack.

subroutines.name: fp_eval_cont_frac
subroutines.title: Evaluate a continued-fraction approximation
subroutines.description: A (low) and Y (high) point at a coefficient table: a count byte followed by that many five-byte fp coefficients. The argument is stashed in TEMP1; the routine folds the table from the top, alternating FWA = arg / FWA and FWA = FWA + next coefficient, to evaluate the continued fraction used by the trig kernels.

subroutines.name: fwa_complement_half_pi
subroutines.title: FWA = pi/2 - FWA
subroutines.description: Subtract FWA from pi/2 using the two-part constant at &AA59/&AA5E for extra precision, then negate. Used for ACS (pi/2 - ASN) and the large-argument arctan identity atn(x) = pi/2 - atn(1/x).

subroutines.name: sin_cos_reduce
subroutines.title: Range-reduce the SIN/COS argument
subroutines.description: Divide the argument by pi/2 to find the quadrant (stored in &4A) and Cody-Waite reduce to the principal range using the two-part pi/2 constant, leaving the reduced angle in FWA for the series. Errors if the argument is too large to reduce.

on_exit.BRK: on an argument too large to reduce

subroutines.name: point_half_pi_hi
subroutines.title: Point the fp pointer at the high part of pi/2 (&AA59)
subroutines.description: Set zp_fp_ptr to the high half of the extended-precision pi/2 constant, ready to unpack.

subroutines.name: point_half_pi_lo
subroutines.title: Point the fp pointer at the low part of pi/2 (&AA5E)
subroutines.description: Set zp_fp_ptr to the low half of the extended-precision pi/2 constant, ready to unpack.

subroutines.name: point_const_half_pi
subroutines.title: Point the fp pointer at the pi/2 constant (&AA63)
subroutines.description: Set zp_fp_ptr to the packed pi/2 constant, ready to unpack (used by the range reduction in SIN/COS).

subroutines.name: fwa_int_power
subroutines.title: FWA = FWA ^ n (n in A)
subroutines.description: Raise FWA to an integer power by repeated multiplication; a negative exponent reciprocates first. Used by EXP to form e^(integer part).

on_exit.zp_fwa (&2E-&35): FWA raised to the n-th power

subroutines.name: ascii_to_number
subroutines.title: Convert an ASCII number to a value
subroutines.description:
Convert the ASCII number in the string work area to a value:
an integer in IWA or a real in FWA. Underlies VAL and numeric
tokenising. A binary zero is appended to the SWA.

on_entry.zp_strbuf_len (&36): length of the ASCII number in the SWA

subroutines.name: iwa_abs
subroutines.title: Make the integer accumulator positive
subroutines.description: IWA = ABS(IWA).

subroutines.name: fwa_negate
subroutines.title: FWA = -FWA
subroutines.description: Negate FWA by flipping the sign bit (a zero value is left unchanged).

on_exit.A: real type marker (&FF)

subroutines.name: iwa_negate
subroutines.title: Negate the integer accumulator
subroutines.description: IWA = -IWA (two's-complement negate the 32-bit integer).

subroutines.name: read_string_literal
subroutines.title: Read a string literal
subroutines.description: Read a quoted ("...", with "" for a literal quote) or unquoted string at PtrB into the string buffer.

subroutines.name: eval_factor
subroutines.title: Evaluate a factor (evaluator level 1)
subroutines.description:
Evaluate the highest-precedence level of an expression at PtrB:
unary minus, unary plus and NOT; parenthesised sub-expressions;
the ?, !, $ and | indirection operators; string literals; and the
built-in functions. Unlike the higher levels, it does not read the
trailing operator - the caller does that.

on_entry.zp_text_ptr2 (&19/&1A): PtrB at the factor to evaluate
on_entry.zp_text_ptr2_off (&1B): offset into the text

on_exit.A: result type: <0 = float in fwa, >0 = integer in iwa, 0 = string
on_exit.zp_iwa / zp_fwa / string_work (&0600): the value, selected by the type in A

subroutines.name: int_result_a
subroutines.title: Return A as an integer result
subroutines.description: Set IWA to the unsigned byte in A (high bytes zero) and report the integer type. The common tail for functions returning a small integer.

on_exit.A: integer type marker (&40)

subroutines.name: iwa_from_ya
subroutines.title: Set the integer accumulator to a small integer
subroutines.description: IWA = 256*Y + A, zero-extended to 32 bits (unsigned 0-65535), then report the integer type.

on_exit.A: integer type marker (&40)

subroutines.name: rnd_range
subroutines.title: RND(X), X>=2: a random integer 1 to X
subroutines.description:
Compute 1 + INT(RND(1) * X). int_to_fwa(X), push X
(stack_real), take a fraction (rnd_fraction), pop X back into the
operand (unstack_real), multiply (fwa_mul_var_raw), convert to an
integer (fwa_to_int) and add one (iwa_inc), giving a value in 1..X.

subroutines.name: rnd_seed
subroutines.title: RND(-X): seed the generator and return X
subroutines.description:
Store the integer argument into the 32-bit state (&0D-&10) and
set &11 to &40. Only bit 0 of &11 is part of the LFSR, so the
overflow bit becomes 0. Returns the argument as an integer.

on_exit.A: integer type marker (&40)

subroutines.name: rnd_integer
subroutines.title: RND: a full-range random integer
subroutines.description:
Bare RND. Advance the generator (rnd_step) then read the
32-bit state (&0D-&10) into IWA as a signed integer, returning the
integer type (&40). The result spans the full signed 32-bit range.

on_exit.A: integer type marker (&40)

subroutines.name: iwa_load_zp
subroutines.title: Load a zero-page integer variable into the accumulator
subroutines.description: Copy the 4-byte integer at &00+X (a resident integer variable A%-Z% or @%) into IWA, then report the integer type.

on_entry.X: zero-page offset of the source variable (from &00)

on_exit.A: integer type marker (&40)

subroutines.name: rnd_fraction
subroutines.title: RND(1): a random real in [0, 1)
subroutines.description:
Advance the generator (rnd_step) then fall into rnd_repeat to
build the fraction. The value is the byte-reversed 32-bit state
divided by 2^32: a real in [0, 1).

on_exit.A: real type marker (&FF)

subroutines.name: rnd_repeat
subroutines.title: RND(0): repeat the last RND(1) without advancing
subroutines.description:
Build the floating-point fraction from the CURRENT generator
state WITHOUT stepping it (so RND(0) repeats the last RND(1)). The
mantissa bytes m1..m4 are loaded most-significant-first from the
little-endian state &0D,&0E,&0F,&10, which byte-reverses the 32-bit
value; with exponent &80 and after normalisation the result is
state-reversed / 2^32, a real in [0, 1).

on_exit.A: real type marker (&FF)

subroutines.name: rnd_step
subroutines.title: Advance the random-number LFSR by 32 steps
subroutines.description:
Advance the 33-bit linear-feedback shift register in
zp_rnd_seed by exactly 32 single-bit steps (one RND call). The
register uses the primitive trinomial x^33 + x^20 + 1, i.e. taps
(33, 20, 0): each step feeds (bit 19 XOR bit 32) into bit 0 and
shifts the whole register left by one.

Per step: take byte 2 (bits 16-23), shift right 3 so bit 19 reaches
bit 0, EOR with byte 4 to bring in bit 32, rotate to put the feedback
bit into carry, then ROL the five state bytes so carry enters bit 0.

subroutines.name: read_key_timed
subroutines.title: Read a key within a time limit (INKEY)
subroutines.description: Evaluate the time-limit argument at PtrB into IWA, then tail-call OSBYTE &81 (INKEY) to read a key with that timeout. Shared by the INKEY and INKEY$ functions.

on_exit.X: the key code (or internal key number on a scan)
on_exit.Y: 0 if a key was read, &FF on timeout, &1B on Escape

subroutines.name: find_def
subroutines.title: Find a PROC/FN definition by name
subroutines.description: Walk the program from PAGE for the DEF PROC/FN line whose name matches the reference at (zp_general), then cache its body address in the variable entry so later calls resolve quickly. Raises No such FN/PROC.

on_exit.the variable entry: updated with the cached body address
on_exit.BRK: No such FN/PROC if no matching DEF exists

subroutines.name: call_proc_fn
subroutines.title: Enter a PROC or FN
subroutines.description:
The procedure/function call mechanism. First copies the live
6502 hardware stack onto the BASIC value stack and resets the
hardware stack -- this is how BBC BASIC lets PROCs and FNs nest far
beyond the 256-byte 6502 stack. Then pushes the call context (the
PROC/FN token and the caller's text pointers), locates the named
definition, binds any parameters, and transfers control to the body.
ENDPROC / =expr unwind this frame.

Note what is NOT saved: only the 6502 hardware stack and the BASIC
value stack are preserved across the call. The FOR, REPEAT and GOSUB
stacks (page 5, counters &24/&25/&26) are global and untouched. So a
loop opened inside the body and left via an early ENDPROC (or GOTO)
leaks its frame -- the counter is never decremented. See stmt_endproc
(&9356) and the page-5 control-flow stacks at &0500.

subroutines.name: stack_local
subroutines.title: Save a variable for LOCAL
subroutines.description:
Push a variable's current value and identity (address and type)
onto the BASIC stack so ENDPROC can restore it, implementing LOCAL.

on_exit.zp_stack_ptr (&04/&05): lowered past the saved value and identity

subroutines.name: iwa_load_var
subroutines.title: Load an integer variable into the accumulator
subroutines.description: Copy the 4-byte integer addressed by zp_iwa into IWA.

on_entry.zp_iwa (&2A/&2B): a pointer to the 4-byte integer variable

subroutines.name: load_byte_var
subroutines.title: Load a byte variable into IWA
subroutines.description: Read the byte addressed by zp_iwa and return it as an unsigned integer in IWA (via iwa_from_ya). Part of the typed variable loader.

on_entry.(zp_iwa) (&2A/&2B): a pointer to the byte variable

on_exit.A: integer type marker (&40)

subroutines.name: load_real_var
subroutines.title: Load a real variable into FWA
subroutines.description: Unpack the 5-byte packed real addressed by zp_iwa (used here as a pointer to the variable) into FWA, restoring the implied leading 1 unless the value is zero. Part of the typed variable loader.

subroutines.name: load_string_var
subroutines.title: Load a string variable into the buffer
subroutines.description: Copy a string variable into the string buffer. A normal string variable carries its pointer and length in the descriptor at (zp_iwa); a $-string (Y = &80) is read from (zp_iwa) up to the terminating CR. Part of the typed variable loader.

on_entry.(zp_iwa) (&2A/&2B): a pointer to the string variable

subroutines.name: find_error_line
subroutines.title: Find the line an error occurred in
subroutines.description: Walk the program from PAGE to locate the line whose text span contains the current program pointer and record its number in ERL (0 in immediate mode).

on_entry.zp_text_ptr (&0B/&0C): the program pointer at the error
on_entry.zp_page (&18): PAGE, the start of the program

subroutines.name: assign_number
subroutines.title: Store a numeric value into a variable
subroutines.description:
Assign the current numeric value (integer or real) to the
variable whose data address is on the stack, coercing to the variable's
own type (real variables get the value converted to FP).

subroutines.name: iwa_store_var
subroutines.title: Store the accumulator into an integer variable
subroutines.description: Copy IWA into the 4-byte integer variable addressed by &37.

on_entry.(zp_general) (&37/&38): a pointer to the integer variable

subroutines.name: print_token
subroutines.title: De-tokenise and print a character or token
subroutines.description: Print A directly if below &80, otherwise look the token up in the keyword table at &8071 and print its expanded keyword text.

on_entry.A: the character or keyword token to print

subroutines.name: print_hex_byte
subroutines.title: Print A as two hex digits
subroutines.description: Print the byte in A as two hex digits (high nibble then low) via print_hex_digit / print_char.

on_exit.A: corrupted

subroutines.name: print_hex_digit
subroutines.title: Print the low nibble of A as a hex digit
subroutines.description: Convert A (0-15) to the ASCII hex digit 0-9 / A-F and fall into print_char to emit it with column tracking.

on_entry.A: a value 0-15 (the low nibble to print)

on_exit.A: corrupted

subroutines.name: print_char
subroutines.title: Print a character with column tracking
subroutines.description: Output A through the print formatter, handling CR specially and maintaining the print column COUNT. Auto-newlines when the column reaches WIDTH.

subroutines.name: print_space
subroutines.title: Print a space through the print formatter
subroutines.description: Print a space via print_char, auto-newlining when the column reaches WIDTH.

on_exit.A: space (&20)

subroutines.name: print_listo_indent
subroutines.title: Print LISTO indentation
subroutines.description: If the LISTO flag bit in A is set against zp_listo, print 2*X spaces of indentation for a LIST line.

on_entry.A: the LISTO bit mask to test

subroutines.name: stmt_listo
subroutines.title: LISTO - set the listing options
subroutines.description: Evaluate the option value and store it in the LISTO flag (&1F), which controls LIST indentation and spacing. LISTO n.

on_entry.zp_text_ptr_off (&0A): offset just past the keyword token

on_exit.control: rejoins statement_loop

subroutines.name: find_line_target
subroutines.title: Resolve a line-number operand to a program line
subroutines.description:
Read a line-number argument (an embedded tokenised line number
or an evaluated integer expression) and locate that line in the
program via find_program_line. Used by GOTO, GOSUB and RESTORE.
Raises "No such line" if absent.

on_entry.zp_text_ptr_off (&0A): offset at the line-number operand

on_exit.(zp_fwb_exp) (&3D/&3E): a pointer to the target line
on_exit.BRK: No such line if the line is absent

subroutines.name: next_data_item
subroutines.title: Advance the DATA pointer to the next item
subroutines.description: Move the DATA pointer past the current item to the next comma- or DATA-separated value, searching forward through the program for the next DATA statement at end of line; raises Out of DATA.

on_exit.zp_text_ptr2 (&19/&1A): PtrB at the next DATA value
on_exit.BRK: Out of DATA if none remain

subroutines.name: read_input_line
subroutines.title: Print the prompt and read a line
subroutines.description: Print the character in A as a prompt, then read a line into the input buffer at &0700 via OSWORD 0.

on_exit.C: set if the read was terminated by Escape

subroutines.name: delete_program_line
subroutines.title: Delete a program line and close the gap
subroutines.description: Find the line (find_program_line), then shift every later line down over it and update TOP. No-op if the line is absent.

on_exit.the program: the line removed and memory compacted
on_exit.zp_top (&12/&13): TOP reduced by the deleted line

subroutines.name: clear_vars_heap_stack
subroutines.title: Clear all variables, the heap and the stack
subroutines.description: Reset LOMEM and VARTOP to TOP, empty the per-letter variable table, and clear the DATA pointer and the BASIC stacks (NEW/CLEAR/RUN).

on_entry.zp_top (&12/&13): TOP, the end of the program

subroutines.name: stack_real
subroutines.title: Push the floating-point accumulator onto the BASIC stack
subroutines.description:
Reserve five bytes on the BASIC stack and copy the packed
floating-point accumulator onto it. Errors if the stack meets the heap.

subroutines.name: unstack_real
subroutines.title: Pop a real off the BASIC stack
subroutines.description:
Point zp_fp_ptr at the 5-byte real on top of the BASIC stack
and drop it (advance the stack pointer by 5), so an FP routine can
use the popped value as its (zp_fp_ptr) operand.

on_entry.(zp_stack_ptr) (&04/&05): a packed 5-byte real on top of stack

subroutines.name: stack_value
subroutines.title: Push the current value by type
subroutines.description:
Push the current value onto the BASIC stack in the form
matching its type: a string (stack_string), a real (stack_real) or,
falling through, an integer (stack_integer).

on_entry.A: value type: 0 string, negative real, else integer
on_entry.zp_iwa / zp_fwa / string_work (&0600): the value, selected by the type in A

subroutines.name: stack_integer
subroutines.title: Push the integer accumulator onto the BASIC stack
subroutines.description:
Reserve four bytes on the BASIC stack (zp_stack_ptr, &04) and
copy the integer accumulator (zp_iwa) onto it. Errors if the stack
would collide with the heap.

subroutines.name: stack_string
subroutines.title: Push the current string onto the BASIC stack
subroutines.description:
Copy the string from the string buffer (length zp_strbuf_len,
&36; text at &0600) onto the BASIC stack, length last. Errors if the
stack meets the heap.

subroutines.name: unstack_string
subroutines.title: Pop a string off the BASIC stack
subroutines.description: Copy the length-prefixed string on top of the stack into the string buffer and drop it (advance the stack pointer past the length byte and the characters).

on_entry.(zp_stack_ptr) (&04/&05): a length-prefixed string on top of stack

subroutines.name: unstack_integer
subroutines.title: Pop an integer from the BASIC stack
subroutines.description:
Copy the four-byte integer on top of the BASIC stack into the
integer accumulator (zp_iwa), then fall into drop_stack_integer to
advance the stack pointer past it.

on_entry.(zp_stack_ptr) (&04/&05): a 4-byte integer (MSB first) on top of stack

subroutines.name: drop_stack_integer
subroutines.title: Drop an integer off the stack
subroutines.description: Advance the BASIC stack pointer by four bytes.

subroutines.name: unstack_int_to_zp
subroutines.title: Pop a stacked integer into a zero-page variable
subroutines.description:
Copy the four-byte integer on top of the BASIC stack into
the zero-page bytes at (X .. X+3), then drop it from the stack. The
&BE0B entry uses X = &37 (the general work area).

on_entry.(zp_stack_ptr) (&04/&05): a 4-byte integer on top of stack

subroutines.name: reserve_stack
subroutines.title: Set the stack pointer and check for room
subroutines.description:
Set the BASIC value-stack pointer to a new top (low byte in A,
borrow already taken into the high byte) and check it has not run
down into the top of variable storage (zp_vartop). Raises "No room"
on collision; otherwise returns with the stack lowered.

subroutines.name: iwa_store_zp
subroutines.title: Store the accumulator into a zero-page integer variable
subroutines.description: Copy IWA into the 4-byte resident integer variable at &00+X (the inverse of iwa_load_zp).

subroutines.name: load_program
subroutines.title: Load a program from the filing system
subroutines.description: Set the OSFILE load address to PAGE and call OSFILE &FF to read the named BASIC program into memory. Used by LOAD and CHAIN.

on_entry.the filename block: set up for OSFILE (control block at &37)

subroutines.name: check_program
subroutines.title: Validate the program and set TOP
subroutines.description: Walk the lines from PAGE checking each is well-formed (CR-terminated, non-zero length), and set TOP past the end. Raises "Bad program" if a line is malformed.

on_entry.zp_page (&18): PAGE, the start of the program

on_exit.zp_top (&12/&13): TOP, just past the last line
on_exit.BRK: Bad program if a line is malformed

subroutines.name: eval_channel
subroutines.title: Evaluate a #channel argument
subroutines.description: Require "#" at PtrB then evaluate the file handle as an integer, leaving it in IWA. Raises Missing # if the "#" is absent.

on_exit.A: the handle low byte

banners.name: language_entry
banners.title: Sideways ROM header — language-entry slot (3 bytes)
banners.description:
MOS dispatches ``JMP &8000`` on language startup.

Byte 0 is ``&c9`` — the language entry is **inline code**, not a ``JMP abs``. This ROM declares itself a language (``rom_type`` bit 6 set) and the MOS enters by *calling* &8000 directly, so the bytes are executed in place (e.g. BBC BASIC's ``CMP #1 / BEQ / RTS``).

Reason code in A on entry:

| A | Meaning                                              |
|---|------------------------------------------------------|
| 0 | No language available — MOS calling Tube ROM         |
| 1 | Normal startup                                       |
| 2 | Request next byte of softkey expansion (Electron)    |
| 3 | Request length of softkey expansion (Electron)       |

banners.name: rom_type
banners.title: ROM identification
banners.description: Six descriptive fields that follow the entry-point JMPs: ROM type flag byte, copyright-string offset, binary version, title string, optional version string, and copyright string. The MOS uses these for identification, dispatch-table lookup, and the ``(C)``-prefix validity check.

banners.name: rom_type
banners.title: ROM type byte
banners.description:
| Bit | Value | Meaning                         |
|-----|------|---------------------------------|
| 7   | 0    | No service entry                |
| 6   | 1    | Language entry present          |
| 5   | 1    | Tube relocation address present |
| 4   | 0    | No Electron firmkey             |
| 3-0 | 0000 | Processor: 6502 BASIC           |

memory_map.name: zp_lomem
memory_map.description: LOMEM: the start of variable storage. Defaults to [`TOP`](address:0012) when a program is run and grows upward as variables are created (tracked by [`VARTOP`](address:0002)); settable with `LOMEM=`.

memory_map.name: zp_vartop
memory_map.description: VARTOP: the address just past the last allocated variable — where the next new variable goes. Runs from [`LOMEM`](address:0000) up toward [`zp_stack_ptr`](address:0004); the two meeting is "No room".

memory_map.name: zp_stack_ptr
memory_map.description:
Pointer to the top of BASIC's value stack, which grows
downward from [`HIMEM`](address:0006). The stack carries expression
temporaries, strings pushed during evaluation, and — across a PROC/FN
call — a saved copy of the 6502 hardware stack. "No room" is raised if
it descends to meet [`VARTOP`](address:0002).

memory_map.name: zp_himem
memory_map.description: HIMEM: the top of memory available to BASIC. The value stack ([`zp_stack_ptr`](address:0004)) grows down from here; settable with `HIMEM=`. Read from the MOS (OSBYTE &84) at startup.

memory_map.name: zp_erl
memory_map.description: ERL: the line number in which the last error occurred, set by the error handler and read by the `ERL` function (0 in immediate mode).

memory_map.name: zp_text_ptr_off
memory_map.description: PtrA offset: the offset of the next character within the program line addressed by [`zp_text_ptr`](address:000b). The interpreter reads the tokenised program through this pointer/offset pair.

memory_map.name: zp_text_ptr
memory_map.description: PtrA: the primary program/text pointer — the base address of the line currently being interpreted. Combined with [`zp_text_ptr_off`](address:000a) to fetch tokens.

memory_map.name: zp_rnd_seed
memory_map.description: RND state: the 33-bit linear-feedback shift register behind `RND`. &0D-&10 are a little-endian 32-bit value (bits 0-31); bit 0 of &11 is bit 32. Advanced 32 steps per `RND` call; reseeded by `RND(-n)`.

memory_map.name: zp_top
memory_map.description: TOP: the address just past the end of the tokenised program text (and the default for [`LOMEM`](address:0000)). Read by the `TOP` pseudo-variable and set when the program is edited.

memory_map.name: zp_print_bytes
memory_map.description: Print field width: the @%-derived field width used while formatting a number for `PRINT`, counting the characters emitted so far in the current field.

memory_map.name: zp_print_flag
memory_map.description: Number-base flag for output: 0 selects decimal, negative selects hexadecimal (set by `STR$~` and `PRINT ~`).

memory_map.name: zp_error_vec
memory_map.description: `ON ERROR` handler address: where the interpreter jumps when an error is trapped. Reset to the default handler (&B433) at the start of each statement line; set by `ON ERROR`.

memory_map.name: zp_page
memory_map.description: PAGE high byte: the start of the BASIC program as a page number (PAGE = this × 256). Read from OSHWM at startup; the `PAGE` pseudo-variable exposes it.

memory_map.name: zp_text_ptr2
memory_map.description: PtrB: the secondary text pointer, used by the expression evaluator and the tokeniser while [`zp_text_ptr`](address:000b) (PtrA) is preserved. Paired with [`zp_text_ptr2_off`](address:001b).

memory_map.name: zp_text_ptr2_off
memory_map.description: PtrB offset: the offset of the next character for the secondary text pointer [`zp_text_ptr2`](address:0019).

memory_map.name: zp_data_ptr
memory_map.description: DATA pointer: the address of the next `DATA` item to be `READ`. Set to the program start by `RESTORE` (or a line by `RESTORE n`) and advanced as items are read.

memory_map.name: zp_count
memory_map.description: COUNT: the number of characters printed since the last newline (the print column). Used for `,` field alignment and the `WIDTH` auto-newline; read by the `COUNT` function.

memory_map.name: zp_listo
memory_map.description: LISTO flags: the listing-indent options set by `LISTO n`. Control whether `LIST` indents `FOR`/`REPEAT` bodies and spaces tokens.

memory_map.name: zp_trace_flag
memory_map.description: TRACE flag: &00 = tracing off, &FF = on (`TRACE ON`/`TRACE OFF`). When on, executed line numbers are printed in brackets up to [`zp_trace_max`](address:0021).

memory_map.name: zp_trace_max
memory_map.description: TRACE ceiling: the highest line number that `TRACE` reports (`TRACE n`). Lines above it are not traced.

memory_map.name: zp_width
memory_map.description: WIDTH: the print line width for the auto-newline (`WIDTH n`). &FF (the default) means no automatic wrap; compared against [`zp_count`](address:001e).

memory_map.name: zp_repeat_level
memory_map.description: REPEAT stack depth: how many `REPEAT` loops are open, indexing [`repeat_stack`](address:05a4). Not saved across a PROC/FN call, so leaving a loop via `ENDPROC`/`GOTO` leaks its entry.

memory_map.name: zp_gosub_level
memory_map.description: GOSUB stack depth: how many `GOSUB` calls are open, indexing [`gosub_stack`](address:05cc). Not saved across a PROC/FN call.

memory_map.name: zp_for_level
memory_map.description: FOR stack depth: how many `FOR` loops are open (×15 gives the byte offset into [`for_stack`](address:0500)). Not saved across a PROC/FN call.

memory_map.name: zp_var_type
memory_map.description: Value type of the most recently fetched/evaluated value: 0 = string, positive (&40) = integer, negative = real. Drives the type dispatch throughout the evaluator.

memory_map.name: zp_opt_flag
memory_map.description: Inline-assembler OPT flags (`OPT n`): bit 0 prints a listing, bit 1 enables error reporting, bit 2 assembles to the offset address O% instead of P%. &FF outside `[ ]` marks "not assembling".

memory_map.name: zp_asm_opcode
memory_map.description: Inline assembler: the opcode byte being built for the instruction currently being assembled, before its operand bytes are appended.

memory_map.name: zp_iwa
memory_map.description: IWA — the 32-bit integer work accumulator. Holds the integer operand/result of the evaluator and the integer arithmetic primitives, and doubles as a pointer to a variable’s value during a fetch.

memory_map.name: zp_fwa_sign
memory_map.description: FWA — floating-point work accumulator A (&2E-&35): sign (&2E), overflow/guard (&2F), excess-128 exponent (&30), 32-bit mantissa MSB-first (&31-&34) and a rounding byte (&35). The main register for real arithmetic; see [`zp_fwb_sign`](address:003b).

memory_map.name: zp_strbuf_len
memory_map.description: Length of the string currently in the string work area at [`string_work`](address:0600).

memory_map.name: zp_general
memory_map.description: General-purpose work pointer (&37-&3A). Reused widely — the tokeniser/line scanner, the variable-chain walk, the program editor — as a scratch 16-bit pointer.

memory_map.name: zp_fileblk
memory_map.description: Filing-system control block (&39 onward): the OSFILE / load-save parameter block. Filing is not active during arithmetic, so this overlaps the FP workspace below ([`zp_fwb_sign`](address:003b) onward).

memory_map.name: zp_fwb_sign
memory_map.description: FWB — floating-point work accumulator B (&3B-&42), same layout as [`zp_fwa_sign`](address:002e). Supplies the second operand for binary floating-point operations (add, multiply, divide).

memory_map.name: zp_fwb_ovf
memory_map.description: FWB overflow byte (&3C), part of [`zp_fwb_sign`](address:003b). A much-reused scratch location: outside floating point it serves as the tokeniser's line-number-arming flag (&FF after GOTO etc. means encode the next number as a line number), a variable-chain walk pointer, an array dimension-descriptor offset, and LIST's REPEAT/UNTIL indent counter. Read each use in its own context rather than assuming a single global meaning.

memory_map.name: zp_fp_temp
memory_map.description: Floating-point temporary / scratch (&43-&47). Holds spill bytes for the FP routines and serves as the quotient build area for integer `DIV`/`MOD`.

memory_map.name: zp_dp_flag
memory_map.description: Decimal-point-seen flag, set while parsing a number literal so a second `.` is rejected and the fractional digits are scaled.

memory_map.name: zp_dec_exp
memory_map.description: Decimal exponent accumulated while parsing the `E` part of a real literal.

memory_map.name: zp_int_exp
memory_map.description: Integer-part / exponent scratch: holds the integer exponent during number parsing and the integer part of a value in `EXP` and the int/fraction split.

memory_map.name: zp_fp_ptr
memory_map.description: Pointer to a packed 5-byte floating-point value (a variable or an FP temporary) for the pack/unpack routines.

memory_map.name: zp_coeff_ptr
memory_map.description: Pointer to the current coefficient table, used while evaluating the continued-fraction approximations in the trig functions.

memory_map.name: zp_error_ptr
memory_map.description: Pointer to the error block currently being reported (the bytes after a `BRK`): the error number and message the handler is processing.

memory_map.name: zp_escflg
memory_map.description: ESCFLG — the MOS escape flag. Its top bit is set when Escape is pressed; BASIC polls it between statements, acknowledges it via OSBYTE, and raises the "Escape" error.

memory_map.name: hw_stack
memory_map.description: The 6502 hardware stack (page 1), used normally for JSR/RTS and register saves. A PROC/FN call copies a snapshot of the live stack onto the BASIC value stack and restores it on return, so call nesting is bounded by free stack space rather than a fixed table.

memory_map.name: resint_at
memory_map.description: The resident integer variables, four bytes each: @% here at &0400, then A%-Z% at &0404-&046B. @% sets the `PRINT`/`STR$` number format. Two slots double as the inline assembler's counters — O% (the 'O' slot, &043C) is the offset-assembly address and P% (the 'P' slot, &0440) the program counter.

memory_map.name: fp_temp1
memory_map.description: Four 5-byte packed floating-point temporaries: TEMP1 (&046C), TEMP2 (&0471), TEMP3 (&0476) and TEMP4 (&047B). The maths routines stash intermediate reals here while reusing [`FWA`](address:002e) / [`FWB`](address:003b).

memory_map.name: var_ptr_table
memory_map.description: The dynamic-variable chain-head table: a two-byte head pointer per initial-character class (A-Z, a-z, `_`, `@`), addressed as &0400 + 2×char. find_variable walks the linked list of variables sharing an initial character; create_variable links a new one in at the head.

memory_map.name: for_stack
memory_map.description: The `FOR` stack: up to 10 frames of 15 bytes each (&0500-&0595; depth in [`zp_for_level`](address:0026)). Each frame holds the control-variable address and type, the STEP and limit values, and the loop-back text pointer — pushed by `FOR`, consulted and updated by `NEXT`.

memory_map.name: repeat_stack
memory_map.description: `REPEAT` loop-start text pointers, low bytes — up to 20 nested (depth in [`zp_repeat_level`](address:0024)); high bytes in [`repeat_stack_hi`](address:05b8). `UNTIL` reloads the pointer to re-test its condition.

memory_map.name: repeat_stack_hi
memory_map.description: High bytes of the `REPEAT` loop-start pointers; low bytes in [`repeat_stack`](address:05a4).

memory_map.name: gosub_stack
memory_map.description: `GOSUB` return text pointers, low bytes — up to 26 nested (depth in [`zp_gosub_level`](address:0025)); high bytes in [`gosub_stack_hi`](address:05e6). `RETURN` pops the top entry.

memory_map.name: gosub_stack_hi
memory_map.description: High bytes of the `GOSUB` return pointers; low bytes in [`gosub_stack`](address:05cc).

memory_map.name: string_work
memory_map.description: The string work area / `CALL` parameter block. BASIC builds string results here — the text of `STR$`, the digits of a number conversion, a popped string for comparison — with the length in [`zp_strbuf_len`](address:0036). `CALL` also assembles its parameter block here.

memory_map.name: line_input_buf
memory_map.description: The line input buffer. The line editor reads a typed line (at the `>` prompt or for `INPUT`) into here via OSWORD 0; the tokeniser then processes it in place.

comments_before_label:
Divide FWA by ten. In binary 1/10 = 0.000110011001100...,
so x/10 is x/16 plus a series of progressively shifted terms.
Each term is built in FWB as a byte/bit-shifted copy of the
mantissa and accumulated into FWA. The result is unnormalised
and unrounded (the caller normalises).
